frameworks.name: noise correlations in population coding
frameworks.one_line: correlated variability shaping cortical population codes across timescales

frameworks.name: cortical laminar circuitry
frameworks.one_line: layer-specific input sources and local connectivity as a structural probe

core_question: Do cortical layers with distinct input sources and local circuitry generate different patterns of neuronal correlations?
key_assumptions: laminar variation in inputs and microcircuitry maps onto functionally distinct subnetworks
correlations on different timescales reflect different underlying mechanisms
key_findings: slow timescale correlations prominent in superficial and deep V1 layers, near zero in middle layers
brief timescale synchrony slightly stronger in middle V1 layers but present at most depths
LFP power shows complementary laminar pattern for low frequency (<10 Hz) vs gamma (30-50 Hz)
V2 shows similar laminar synchrony pattern as V1 but slow correlations do not differ between input and nearby layers
interpretations: cortical circuits in different laminae can produce remarkably different correlation patterns despite tight interconnection
